questions.q: What are the 3 ways to send signals to Meta? Select 3.
questions.explanation: Pixel, CAPI and SDK are the 3 main data sources. Offline events are sent via the CAPI Offline channel. Customer List is for audiences, not signals.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Custom Event and a Custom Conversion?
options: A Custom Event is a NEW event defined by the business (requires code). A Custom Conversion is a RULE on an existing event (no code)
A Custom Event is web only; a Custom Conversion is offline only
A Custom Conversion replaces the Pixel
questions.explanation: Custom Event = new code. Custom Conversion = a rule on an existing event (URL contains, value, etc.).

questions.q: After iOS 14+, why does CAPI become more important?
options: ATT requires using CAPI by law
ATT limits Pixel tracking in iOS browsers without consent. CAPI sends server-side and is not affected
The Pixel stops working entirely on iOS
questions.explanation: ATT asks for explicit consent on iOS. Most users decline, limiting the Pixel. CAPI server-side maintains coverage.

questions.q: How many events can be prioritized per web domain under AEM?
questions.explanation: Maximum 8 prioritized events per web domain under Aggregated Event Measurement.

questions.q: At which level of the hierarchy is the dataset connected to a campaign?
questions.explanation: Ad set level, along with the conversion location and conversion event.

questions.q: An advertiser closes many contracts in person at the office. How do they record that conversion?
options: Standard Purchase event with value, sent via the CAPI Offline channel
questions.explanation: Purchase with value via the CAPI Offline channel. A monthly CSV with hashed offline events is uploaded.

options: You send rich hashed customer info (email, phone, IP, user agent) via CAPI
questions.explanation: EMQ improves with the quality of the data sent — CAPI lets you send more hashed info than the Pixel alone.

questions.q: Standard event for a SaaS subscription with recurring billing:
questions.explanation: Subscribe is canonical for a recurring subscription. Purchase is a one-time purchase.

questions.q: Select the parameters Meta RECOMMENDS sending with a Purchase event. Select 4.
questions.explanation: Value, Currency, Content IDs and Content type are the recommended parameters. Full name is NOT sent in plain text — only hashed (and via CAPI).

questions.q: Which statement about AEM is TRUE?
options: AEM removes personal identifiers, adds differential privacy and reports aggregated data
questions.explanation: AEM's 3 actions: 1) removes personal IDs, 2) adds statistical noise (differential privacy), 3) reports in aggregate. Only applies to iOS 14+.
